deployment.name: Public geospatial Q&A — freeform tool returns (example)
deployment.description: The same public-facing geospatial Q&A deployment as the `public-geospatial-qa` preset, but with the agent in FREEFORM tool-response mode: full STAC 1.0 item metadata and per-band raster statistics are serialized straight into the LLM context instead of being held server-side behind a response-template layer. This is the high end of the tool-response-architecture cost lever (paper Tables 4 and 7). It exists so the freeform stress-test row reproduces with no manual anchor overrides — load it, set the public segment MAU, done.

anchor_query.example: Did monthly NO2 over New York City change from October to December 2021?
anchor_query._note: Freeform anchor, measured 2026-05-13 against real OpenAI gpt-5.2 (bench scenario public-geospatial-react-freeform, N=5 session-repeats, 60 LLM calls). The anchor is defined by THREE coordinates: input_tokens 22798, cache_rate_baseline 0.744 (heavy tool-return payloads erode the prompt cache down from the templated preset's 0.88), and tool_response_mode freeform. output_tokens is deliberately held at the templated anchor's 41: the templated-vs-freeform comparison is a controlled lever that varies ONLY the LLM-visible tool-return input. The freeform bench run itself measured ~52 output tokens/turn (1,574 output tokens / 30 user-turns) — answer-length noise worth ~1.3% of monthly cost, immaterial and excluded from the controlled comparison. Reproduction note: all three coordinates must move together; setting only input_tokens and leaving cache at the templated 0.88 understates the freeform cost by ~1.6x.

_calibration.accuracy_statement: N=5 freeform run — an order-of-magnitude anchor for the tool-response-architecture cost lever, not a tight per-coefficient estimate. The paired N=20 templated anchor is in public-geospatial-qa.json.

mixed.label: Mixed (40% full · 30% rag · 15% partial · 10% refusal · 5% heavy)

segments.label: Public visitors (anonymous)
segments.description: Anonymous public users on earth.gov. Set this to 75000 to reproduce the paper's Table 7 stress test.

phases.label: Year 1 — pilot on API
phases.description: Low-risk start. Validate workload + cache hit rate against real usage before committing to spend.

phases.label: Year 2 — committed-spend reservation
phases.description: Lock in a discount with Azure PTU or OpenAI Enterprise commit once volume is steady.

phases.label: Year 3 — hybrid 50/50
phases.description: Stand up Llama-based self-host for steady baseline traffic; keep API for spikes and tail queries.
